events.title: Australia news live: ‘deeply concerned’ Penny Wong says Lebanon should be included in ceasefire and calls for aid workers to be protected
events.description: Foreign affairs minister releases a joint statement with other countries, urging an end to hostilities in Lebanon. Follow updates live Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Shadow defence minister ‘reluctant’ to listen to Iran over Lebanon’s inclusion in ceasefire deal James Paterson , the shadow minister for defence, said he is “reluctant” to listen to Iran on whether or not Lebanon was included in a ceasefire deal announced yesterday as Israel continues to bombard the country. On the one hand, the United States and Israel say that the ceasefire did not include Lebanon, and on the other hand, the Islamic Republic of Iran is saying that it did include Lebanon. I am personally reluctant to endorse claims made by a country which has sponsored terrorist attacks on Australian soil. Australians are seeing what is happening in a number of countries in the Middle East and responding with compassion. We recognise this is a time when Australians are also doing it tough and we appreciate how they are recognising the depth of the humanitarian crisis and responding. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Australia news live: ‘deeply concerned’ Penny Wong says Lebanon should be included in ceasefire and calls for aid workers to be protected

events.title: At least 182 killed as Israel strikes central Beirut after saying Iran truce doesn't apply there
events.description: Lebanon’s health ministry said that Israeli strikes on Wednesday killed 182 people, the highest single-day death toll in the Israel-Hezbollah war. Israel launched a barrage of strikes in central Beirut and elsewhere in the country as a shaky ceasefire took effect between the U.S. and Iran. Iranian officials have maintained that the deal was supposed to include Lebanon, while Israel and the U.S. have insisted that it does not. FRANCE 24's Sharon Gaffney speaks with Sami Nader, Director of the Levant Institute for Strategic Affairs.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 At least 182 killed as Israel strikes central Beirut after saying Iran truce doesn't apply there

events.title: Guterres warns of ‘wider war’ as Middle East conflict enters second month
events.description: The Middle East crisis has lurched into its second month, prompting UN Secretary-General António Guterres to issue a stark warning on Thursday morning that the world is “on the edge of a wider war” with catastrophic global implications.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Guterres warns of ‘wider war’ as Middle East conflict enters second month

events.title: Israel pounds Beirut as Lebanon left out of ceasefire deal
events.description: Lebanon's government said Wednesday Israeli strikes killed dozens and wounded hundreds across the country, with the capital Beirut hit by the most violent bombardment since the start of the war with militant group Hezbollah. It came despite a deal for a two-week ceasefire between the United States and Hezbollah's backer Iran.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Israel pounds Beirut as Lebanon left out of ceasefire deal

events.title: Israel's attacks on Beirut 'much bigger than anything we've seen in this war or the previous one'
events.description: Nadia Massih is pleased to welcome Rania Abouzeid, Lebanese Australian independent journalist and veteran war reporter. Ms. Abouzeid describes "chaotic scenes" in the Lebanese capital. Israel's attacks on Beirut have been extensive and they are now targeting areas well beyond the southern suburbs: "crowded, mixed sectarian residential areas that are full of the displaced". Women and parentless children are flooding hospitals. Amid the "simultaneous boom, boom, boom, strike after strike" there are "massive traffic jams… people just wanted to flee, but flee where?”
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Israel's attacks on Beirut 'much bigger than anything we've seen in this war or the previous one'

events.title: UN nuclear agency chief ‘deeply concerned’ by reports of latest attack on Iran power plant
events.description: Reports of yet another projectile strike near the Bushehr nuclear power plant prompted Rafael Grossi, head of the International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA), to register his deep concern on Saturday.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 UN nuclear agency chief ‘deeply concerned’ by reports of latest attack on Iran power plant

events.title: 3 gunmen open fire outside Israeli consulate in Istanbul, dubbed 'terrorists' by Turkish official
events.description: One attacker is dead and two others injured after a gunfight with police outside the Israeli consulate in Istanbul, Turkey's interior minister says.
events.ticker_label: 💥 3 gunmen open fire outside Israeli consulate in Istanbul, dubbed 'terrorists' by Turkish official

events.title: World held hostage by reliance on fossil fuels, Christiana Figueres warns – and climate health impacts are ‘mother of all injustices’
events.description: Exclusive: Former UN climate chief to co-chair Lancet Commission examining how sea-level rise is reshaping health, wellbeing and inequality What are the health impacts of sea-level rise, and who should pay? Christiana Figueres: Sea-level rise is a health crisis and we must hold polluters accountable Countries are being “held hostage” by their reliance on fossil fuels, a former UN climate chief has warned, describing the health impacts of climate change as “the mother of all injustices”. Christiana Figueres, an international climate negotiator who helped deliver the Paris agreement signed in 2016, made the comments as she was announced on Wednesday as co-chair of a Lancet Commission examining how sea-level rise is reshaping health, wellbeing and inequality . Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 World held hostage by reliance on fossil fuels, Christiana Figueres warns – and climate health impacts are ‘mother of all injustices’

events.title: Iran threatens to end ceasefire over Hezbollah's exclusion from truce deal
events.description: Iran says the U.S. must choose between ceasefire or war via Israel as Tuesday's ceasefire deal excludes Tehran-backed terrorist group Hezbollah.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran threatens to end ceasefire over Hezbollah's exclusion from truce deal

events.title: MIDDLE EAST LIVE 6 April: Strikes persist across region as humanitarian needs rise
events.description: Strikes and counter-strikes continue across the Middle East, with dozens of casualties reported over the weekend in Lebanon following Israeli strikes targeting the south and the capital, Beirut. Meanwhile, humanitarian needs are rising, critical infrastructure remains under strain, and the wider economic and global impacts of the crisis continue to mount. Stay with us for live updates from across the UN system. App users can follow coverage here.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 MIDDLE EAST LIVE 6 April: Strikes persist across region as humanitarian needs rise

events.title: At least 182 killed across Lebanon in large wave of Israeli strikes
events.description: Attacks hit the southern suburbs of Beirut, southern Lebanon and the eastern Bekaa Valley, hours after a US-Iran ceasefire was announced.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 At least 182 killed across Lebanon in large wave of Israeli strikes

events.title: World News in Brief: ‘Skyrocketing’ needs outpace Sudan funding, Ukraine strikes update, global water security
events.description: The UN is significantly scaling up its presence in the Sudanese capital, Khartoum, to expand life-saving operations as the conflict between rival militaries approaches its third year.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 World News in Brief: ‘Skyrocketing’ needs outpace Sudan funding, Ukraine strikes update, global water security

events.title: Cuba energy crisis: Humanitarian needs remain despite fuel supplies
events.description: The UN has issued an urgent call for international support as Cuba grapples with a ‘worsening’ humanitarian crisis fuelled by a prolonged energy blockade and the lingering devastation caused by Hurricane Melissa last year.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Cuba energy crisis: Humanitarian needs remain despite fuel supplies

events.title: World reacts to ‘brutal’ Israeli attacks on Lebanon after US-Iran truce
events.description: Israeli attacks have killed at least 254 people and wounded 1,165 others, according to Lebanon’s Civil Defence.

events.title: Nigeria: At least 20 killed in attack, residents say
events.description: Authorities in Nigeria's restive north put the death toll at three, but residents and humanitarians point to a higher toll. The attack occurred in an area where kidnapping gangs and Islamist militants are active.
events.ticker_label: 🚑 Nigeria: At least 20 killed in attack, residents say

events.title: MIDDLE EAST LIVE 2 April: Civilian impact deepens as UN chief says ‘we must find a peaceful way out’ before region is engulfed by war
events.description: Deadly new strikes reported across the Middle East overnight and Thursday rattled energy markets and pushed crude oil prices up to $107 in early trading. Hopes dwindled of a quick end to the conflict as President Trump spoke of another "two to three weeks" of attacks, alongside "ongoing" discussions with Tehran. Civilians across the region continue to suffer misery and displacement. The UN chief told reporters at UN Headquarters the conflict ‘is already being felt everywhere,” and “the spiral of destruction must stop”. Follow live coverage from the Security Council here. App users can follow coverage here.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 MIDDLE EAST LIVE 2 April: Civilian impact deepens as UN chief says ‘we must find a peaceful way out’ before region is engulfed by war

events.title: Stork warning: woman gives birth midair on Jamaica-to-New York flight
events.description: Baby was delivered during Caribbean Airlines flight from Kingston to the US; nationality of child to be determined Sign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inbox A routine passenger flight from Jamaica landed at New York ’s John F Kennedy international airport with one more person than it took off with after a woman gave birth in midair, potentially setting up a tricky situation over the newborn’s citizenship. The “medical event” occurred on a Caribbean Airlines flight from Kingston on Saturday, according to a news release from the carrier. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: ✈️ Stork warning: woman gives birth midair on Jamaica-to-New York flight

events.title: Energy crisis not over despite relief rally
events.description: Oil prices have plunged and stock markets soared after the announcement of a two-week ceasefire between the US and Israel and Iran. But regardless of whether the deal holds, the energy crisis unleashed by the war is far from over. Damage to infrastructure in the region means it could take years for oil and gas production to get back to pre-war levels. Meanwhile, global aviation group IATA says supplies of jet fuel will remain tight.
events.ticker_label: ✈️ Energy crisis not over despite relief rally

events.title: Oil prices plunge and stocks soar after U.S. and Iran agree on a ceasefire
events.description: Investors around the world breathed a sigh of relief at the prospect of peace — and an easing of the global energy crisis.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Oil prices plunge and stocks soar after U.S. and Iran agree on a ceasefire

events.title: Iran war: how farmers adapt as fertilizer crunch heats up
events.description: The war in Iran has sparked a global fertilizer shortage and is threatening food security. What are farmers around the world doing to save their crops?
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran war: how farmers adapt as fertilizer crunch heats up

events.title: 'Pakistani military calling the shots: Has connections with IRCG, very good relationship with Trump'
events.description: Nadia Massih is pleased to welcome Ali Vaez, Director of Iran Project & Senior Advisor at the International Crisis Group. While the US sees the outcome as a decisive military victory, Vaez argues that the more consequential story lies in the reconfiguration of power within Iran. From Tehran’s perspective, survival in the face of overwhelming force constitutes a form of victory. At the same time, this war has not left Iran unchanged. Power has tilted toward more hardline actors, particularly the Revolutionary Guard, reshaping the internal hierarchy of the Islamic Republic.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 'Pakistani military calling the shots: Has connections with IRCG, very good relationship with Trump'

events.title: Over 1,000 humanitarians have been killed in three years, Security Council hears
events.description: At least 326 humanitarians were killed in the line of duty across 21 countries during 2025, bringing the total killed over three years to over 1,010. The International Red Cross warned the Security Council on Wednesday that “we are losing our humanity in war.”
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Over 1,000 humanitarians have been killed in three years, Security Council hears

events.title: MIDDLE EAST LIVE 8 April: US-Iran ceasefire announced; strikes continue in Lebanon
events.description: After nearly 40 days of intense hostilities across the Middle East – marked by rising civilian casualties and widespread damage to critical infrastructure – a two-week ceasefire between the United States and Iran has been announced. While the UN Secretary-General has welcomed the move as a step toward a broader peace, reports of continued Israeli strikes and mass casualties across Lebanon underscore the fragility of the situation. Stay with us for live updates. App users can follow coverage here.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 MIDDLE EAST LIVE 8 April: US-Iran ceasefire announced; strikes continue in Lebanon

events.title: Mexican miner rescued after nearly 14 days in a flooded Sinaloa mine
events.description: Rescue teams rescued a miner trapped for 13 days after a collapse in Sinaloa, Mexico

events.title: ‘People are afraid’: Lebanese reeling after Israel’s devastating attacks
events.description: Fear and confusion grip Lebanon as Israeli strikes devastate the country and its place in the ceasefire is contested.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 ‘People are afraid’: Lebanese reeling after Israel’s devastating attacks

events.title: Timor-Leste is vulnerable to ‘infiltration by foreign organized crime’, president José Ramos-Horta says
events.description: Australian federal police say they are working with tiny nation to respond to threat of online scam centres Timor-Leste is vulnerable to “infiltration by foreign organized crime”, the country’s president, José Ramos-Horta, has warned. His comments come as Australian federal police confirmed to the Guardian the force is providing support to local law enforcement in Timor-Leste, including a December 2025 visit from the agency’s digital forensic and cyber experts. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Timor-Leste is vulnerable to ‘infiltration by foreign organized crime’, president José Ramos-Horta says

events.title: Can ceasefire hold? US-Iran truce tested before talks start
events.description: No apocalypse. Instead an eleventh hour ceasefire between Iran and the United States, one that is still being tested, two sides that are both claiming victory, and five and-a-half weeks of a war that may change the way the rest of the world looks at both Iran and the United States.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Can ceasefire hold? US-Iran truce tested before talks start

events.description: South Sudan is evolving into a catastrophic human rights and humanitarian crisis, UN Human Rights Council-appointed independent experts warned on Thursday.

events.title: Middle East crisis exposes global energy fault line as UN urges shift to renewables
events.description: The ongoing crisis in the Middle East is exposing a central vulnerability in the global economy: the dependence on fossil fuels flowing through regions affected by conflict, a situation which is strengthening the UN’s case for a faster transition to cheaper, more resilient renewable power.

events.description: The 2026 World Cup final will take place at the MetLife Stadium in New Jersey, a few miles away from UN Headquarters where, on Wednesday, elite athletes and sports administrators spoke about the power of football and other international sports to change the world for the better.

events.title: Trump has found his Iran off-ramp – but at what cost?
events.description: Domestically and internationally, the effects of the war will be felt for years to come.

events.title: Unsanctioned France cricket team plays international tournament amid 'high' legal risks
events.description: France Cricket, the former governing body for the sport in France, is fielding a senior men’s team at an international ranked tournament in Portugal despite having lost its official status on January 1. France's sports ministry warned that doing so would be against the law, and legal experts said those responsible risk civil and criminal liability.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Unsanctioned France cricket team plays international tournament amid 'high' legal risks

events.title: How Trump's threats to Iran have affected the global economy

events.title: France plans 36 billion euro boost to rearmament, nuclear deterrent expansion
events.description: France plans to ⁠add a further 36 ​billion euros to its defence spending between now and 2030 under an updated military planning law that expands its nuclear ​arsenal and boosts missile and drone stocks. The increase reflects mounting security pressures from wars in Ukraine and the Middle East and growing uncertainty over U.S. commitments to NATO under President ​Donald Trump. FRANCE 24's Luke Shrago reports.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 France plans 36 billion euro boost to rearmament, nuclear deterrent expansion

events.title: Pope Leo XIV invokes Pope Francis' final words in Easter plea against growing 'indifference' to war

events.ticker_label: 🪖 Pope Leo XIV invokes Pope Francis' final words in Easter plea against growing 'indifference' to war

events.title: News outlets falsely report Somaliland called for extradition of Ilhan Omar
events.description: Reports, based on X post from unofficial account, follow JD Vance’s accusations and threats of finding ‘legal remedies’ Sign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inbox Several news outlets have falsely reported that Somaliland’s government called for the extradition of Ilhan Omar , basing their stories on a post from an X account that does not represent the state despite its claims to the contrary. Fox News, the New York Post, Sinclair Broadcast Group’s the National News Desk and the Independent ran stories on the US representative. The reports centred on a post by @RepOfSomaliland in reaction to claims by JD Vance that Omar had committed immigration fraud, which echoed prior allegations against the Somali-born Minnesota Democrat that she has vehemently denied. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 News outlets falsely report Somaliland called for extradition of Ilhan Omar

events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Truck drivers warn fuel crisis could see industry ‘grind to a halt’ – as it happened

events.description: Hostilities continue across the Middle East, with ongoing strikes, rising civilian impacts and mounting pressure on critical infrastructure and humanitarian services. On the diplomatic track in New York, China and Russia vetoed a Security Council resolution put forward by Gulf States to ensure safety and security of ships in the Strait of Hormuz - find full coverage here. Meanwhile, displacement remains high, aid access is constrained. Human rights chief Volker Türk says President Trump’s threat to annihilate Iranian civilisation is “sickening”. Stay with us for live updates from across the UN system. App users can follow coverage here.

events.title: New Zealand asks US to send fuel tankers to Pacific to alleviate pressure caused by Iran war
events.description: After meeting with Marco Rubio, foreign minister Winston Peters says he made sure US understands ‘significant economic impacts on New Zealand and Pacific’ Middle East crisis – live updates New Zealand has called on the US to send fuel tankers to the Pacific to help alleviate some of the significant economic and fuel pressure caused by the war in the Middle East. Winston Peters, New Zealand’s foreign minister, met the US secretary of state, Marco Rubio, in Washington on Tuesday, where they discussed bilateral relations, the war in Iran and the Pacific. Continue reading...

events.title: How Trump’s promise to ‘wipe out’ Iran could backfire on US power

events.title: Family pay tribute to British teenager killed in motorcycle crash in Vietnam

The incident occurred on the Ha Giang loop in the country’s north, and Orla Wates, 19, died at the Viet Duc university hospital in Hanoi, according to Viet Nam News. Continue reading...

events.title: Netanyahu says Israel ready to 'return to battle at any moment' against Iran
events.description: Israel's Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said Wednesday that Israel remains prepared to confront Iran if necessary, despite a truce reached between the United States and Iran. Lebanon's civil defence service said 254 ​people had been killed in Israel's strikes across Lebanon on Wednesday. FRANCE 24's Noga Tarnopolsky reports from Jerusalem.

events.title: India's digital census prompts fear of hidden agendas
events.description: The world's most populous country has launched a fully digital census which involves millions of enumerators and also allows the people to report their own data — but many fear it will be used for political manipulation.

events.title: 60 years after the Indonesian mass killings: Is the Cold War back?

FRANCE 24's correspondent in Tehran Reza Sayah says that "we are seeing the first signs of the fragility and the complexity and the confusion surrounding this ceasefire".
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran closes Strait of Hormuz again as Israel pounds Lebanon, air defense systems go off in Tehran

events.title: Security Council: Russia and China veto resolution on Strait of Hormuz

events.title: Albanese’s tactic with Trump has always been don’t buy-in and don’t bite back. Why has that changed?
events.description: The prime minister clearly believed the US president’s threat of mass bombings of bridges and power plants crossed a new line Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Anthony Albanese has adopted a careful and deliberate strategy for dealing with Donald Trump since his return to the White House in early 2025: don’t buy-in, don’t bite back. The approach is a calculation that there is little to be gained from responding to Trump’s every Truth Social post, lest it distract the government, provoke the president or, heaven forbid, threaten the Aukus pact. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 Albanese’s tactic with Trump has always been don’t buy-in and don’t bite back. Why has that changed?

events.title: 'Ketamine Queen' jailed for 15 years over Matthew Perry overdose death

events.title: Trump administration's claims of victory in Iran 'really quite hollow', analyst says

The US can stop its attacks for a while but its claims of victory are really quite hollow".

events.title: Hegseth hails Iran 'victory' as confusion remains over ceasefire deal

But much about the agreement remains unclear with the sides presenting vastly different visions of the terms.

events.title: They bought an abandoned 'ghost house' in the Japanese countryside
events.description: He'd spent years backpacking around the world, and Japanese traveler Daisuke Kajiyama was finally ready to return home to pursue his long-held dream of opening up a guesthouse.

events.title: Tropical Cyclone Vaianu may bring life-threatening winds to New Zealand, forecasters warn
events.description: Category 3 cyclone is moving south of Fiji towards New Zealand, with winds at centre in excess of 150km/h Tropical Cyclone Vaianu forming in the Pacific could bring life-threatening winds and heavy rain to New Zealand later this week, forecasters have said, with strong wind watches issued for the entire North Island. The category 3 cyclone is moving south of Fiji towards New Zealand, with winds around the centre in excess of 150km/h, MetService said on Wednesday. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Tropical Cyclone Vaianu may bring life-threatening winds to New Zealand, forecasters warn

events.title: US seeks to deport Kilmar Ábrego García to Liberia despite new Costa Rica deal
events.description: Man born in El Salvador has been fighting removal to series of ‘third’ countries after mistaken deportation last year US government attorneys on Tuesday told a federal judge the Department of Homeland Security still intends to deport Kilmar Ábrego García to Liberia, despite a new agreement with Costa Rica to accept deportees who cannot legally be returned to their home countries. The Salvadorian national’s case has become a focal point in the immigration debate after he was mistakenly deported to El Salvador last year. Since his return, he has been fighting a second deportation to a series of African countries proposed by homeland security officials. Continue reading...

events.title: Uganda receives first US deportation flight under third-country agreement
events.description: Dozen people arrive under new deal but legal challenges expected with scheme criticised as ‘dehumanising process’ A flight carrying people being deported from the US has landed in Uganda, as Donald Trump’s administration pushes on with its strategy of expelling migrants to countries they have no ties to. The deported people would stay in the east African country as “a transition phase for potential onward transmission to other countries”, an unnamed senior Ugandan government official told Reuters. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Uganda receives first US deportation flight under third-country agreement

This week, Reform UK said they would halt visas for nationals of countries formally demanding reparations from Britain if they took power. Continue reading...

What we are witnessing is not the end of a war, but the emergence of competing narratives that each side must sustain for political legitimacy: both sides claim success, yet neither has truly secured any semblance of victory.

events.title: Chile’s far-right government rips up plan for memorial at Pinochet torture site
events.description: New administration reverses expropriation of property founded by ex-Nazi Paul Schäfer, leaving victims in limbo With its Germanic crosses and colourful toy-town facades, the village square of the tiny Chilean settlement of Villa Baviera gives little indication of the horrors of its past. Until 1991, this cattle town of a few hundred people was a compound known as Colonia Dignidad. Its leader, Paul Schäfer, a former Nazi and weapons smuggler, bought a swathe of land in the valley in 1961, eventually holding as many as 300 people in a fenced enclave with minimal contact with the outside world. He sexually abused and even tortured the children in the camp. Continue reading...

events.title: Cuts to NDIS to be focus of Labor’s quietly launched razor gang ahead of May budget
events.description: Exclusive: Taskforce led by former Treasury official Anthea Long will advise on cost-cutting options for $52bn program Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Labor has quietly established a razor gang to drive budget savings in the national disability insurance scheme, as it works to further rein in costs ahead of next month’s federal budget. An NDIS Sustainability Taskforce was established within the health department earlier this year, with instructions from the federal government and national cabinet to advise on cost-cutting options for the $52bn program. Traoré took power in a coup in September 2022 , toppling another junta that had taken power just nine months earlier. He has since stifled opposition and in January banned political parties outright. Continue reading...

events.title: First Nation asks court to block Alberta referendum on seceding from Canada
events.description: Sturgeon Lake Cree Nation has asked a court to halt the separatist push, arguing it would violate their treaty rights A First Nation in Alberta has said that a separatist push for the province to secede from Canada is “consummately irresponsible and dishonourable” and should be shut down, arguing in court that a proposed referendum would violate their treaty rights. A minority of residents of the oil-rich province have long argued that the province’s woes are due to the structure of payments to the federal government and a perceived inability to get their vast fossil fuel reserves to market. Continue reading...

Crime rates in Toronto as a whole are dropping but residents of Rosedale have been left on edge by a sustained rise in home invasions, with robbers targeting the tree-lined neighbourhood at a rate more than double the city average. Break-ins and thefts remain the third highest per capita in Toronto. Continue reading...

events.title: ‘We still deserve due process,’ says Cambodian man deported by US to Eswatini
events.description: Pheap Rom was one of 15 people sent to prison in African kingdom last year despite completing US sentences A Cambodian man deported by the US said he would have accepted being sent to Cambodia, but instead ended up imprisoned in Eswatini, a country he knew so little about that when he first read the name he thought it was another immigration detention centre in Louisiana. Pheap Rom, who had been convicted of attempted murder, was one of 10 deportees sent to Eswatini by the US in October 2025. They joined a group of five men, from Cambodia, Cuba, Jamaica, Vietnam and Yemen, who were deported to the small southern African country in July. All were sent to a maximum-security prison. Rom was deported from Eswatini to Cambodia in March. Continue reading...

events.description: Vietnam has unanimously elected Communist Party leader To Lam as president, consolidating his control over both party and state

events.title: Car shell suspended on rock face above British Columbia highway in apparent prank
events.description: Authorities in British Columbia have urged people to stay away from a rock face above a highway in the Canadian province where a Volkswagen Beetle shell was suspended in an apparent prank by engineering students

events.title: Germany suspends military approval for long stays abroad for men under 45
events.description: The requirement to obtain permission had gone largely unnoticed until a German newspaper reported on it.

events.title: Look of the Week: Blackpink headline Coachella in Korean hanboks
events.description: Bringing the second day of this year's Coachella to a close, K-Pop girl group Blackpink made history Saturday night when they became the first Asian act to ever headline the festival. To a crowd of, reportedly, over 125,000 people, Jennie, Jisoo, Lisa and Rosé used the ground-breaking moment to pay homage to Korean heritage by arriving onstage in hanboks: a traditional type of dress.

events.title: How Playboy cut ties with Hugh Hefner to create a post-MeToo brand
events.description: Hugh Hefner launched Playboy Magazine 70 years ago this year. The first issue included a nude photograph of Marilyn Monroe, which he had purchased and published without her knowledge or consent.

events.title: Turkey: DW correspondent Alican Uludag still in custody
events.description: Court reporter Alican Uludag has been in pre-trial detention in Istanbul for six weeks, charged with "insulting the president." His lawyers say this is completely disproportionate.

events.title: Greece to ban social media for under-15s from 2027
events.description: Greece will ban access to social media for children under the age of 15 from January 1, 2027, Prime Minister Kyriakos Mitsotakis said on Wednesday, citing rising anxiety, sleep problems and the addictive ⁠design of online platforms.

events.title: Big-box stores could help slash emissions and save millions by putting solar panels on roofs. Why aren't more of them doing it?
events.description: As the US attempts to wean itself off its heavy reliance on fossil fuels and shift to cleaner energy sources, many experts are eyeing a promising solution: your neighborhood big-box stores and shopping malls.

events.title: Greetings from downtown Cairo, where unpretentious cafés are part of centuries-old charm
events.description: Downtown Cairo, or Wust el-Balad as it's known, is a trove of hidden gems. Imprinted on every high-ceilinged building, arched balcony and iconic roundabout are relics that feel like love letters from the past.

events.title: Lawmakers press for probe of Chinese diaspora groups alleged election interference
events.description: Top House lawmakers urge IRS and Treasury to investigate U.S. 'hometown' nonprofits they allege were co-opted by the Chinese Communist Party for political influence.

events.title: Greece to ban social media for under-15s from next year
events.description: It follows similar moves in other European countries, including France and Spain.

events.title: Trump-backed Republican Clay Fuller wins election to replace Marjorie Taylor Greene
events.description: He will serve out the rest of Marjorie Taylor Greene's term, which ends in January 2027.

events.title: Gambia appoints British barrister to prosecute gruesome Jammeh-era crimes
events.description: Ex-President Yahya Jammeh's 22-year rule was known for enforced disappearances and extrajudicial killings.

events.title: Co-founder of Jalisco New Generation drug cartel pleads guilty
events.description: Érick Valencia Salazar was among a group of 29 alleged drug lords extradited to the US from Mexico.

events.title: Newlywed wife of US soldier freed by ICE after detention at military base
events.description: Annie Ramos, an undocumented immigrant who came to the US as a toddler, had gone to the base to complete paperwork so the couple could move in together.

events.title: Man fined after breaking into viral hippo Moo Deng's enclosure
events.description: The director of the Thai zoo where Moo Deng lives said security would be upped after the incident.

events.title: Plan to scrap presidential elections puts Zimbabweans at loggerheads
events.description: The ruling party has unveiled a draft law that strips voters of the chance to elect the president.

events.title: US journalist Shelly Kittleson released after kidnap in Iraq, officials say
events.description: US officials say they are now "working to support her safe departure from Iraq".

events.title: Wireless Festival cancelled after Kanye West blocked from coming to UK
events.description: West was due to headline the festival in July but drew criticism over past antisemitic comments.

events.title: Prosecutors move to subpoena Tiger Woods’ prescription drug records
events.description: Woods pleaded not guilty in his driving under the influence case in Florida last week after rolling his SUV.

events.title: Atletico Madrid shock 10-man Barcelona 2-0 in Champions League quarterfinal
events.description: Atletico take ⁠firm ⁠grip on the quarterfinal with 2-0 first-leg victory as Cubarsi sent off for Barcelona at ​Camp Nou.

events.title: PSG beat Liverpool as Champions League defence maintains pace
events.description: UEFA Champions League holders PSG claim 2-0 first-leg lead over English Premier League champions Liverpool.

events.title: NASA releases picture of ‘Earthset’ shot by Artemis crew
events.description: New NASA 'Earthset' photo from Artemis II echoes Apollo 8’s iconic 'Earthrise' in historic lunar flyby.

events.title: Markets digest bank earnings after recent turmoil
events.description: No description available.

events.title: Still haven't filed your taxes? Here's what you need to know
events.description: So far this tax season, the IRS has received more than 90 million income tax returns for 2022.

events.title: Analysis: Fox News is about to enter the true No Spin Zone
events.description: This is it.

events.title: Scientists identify secret ingredient in Leonardo da Vinci paintings
events.description: "Old Masters" such as Leonardo da Vinci, Sandro Botticelli and Rembrandt may have used proteins, especially egg yolk, in their oil paintings, according to a new study.

events.title: 'A definitive backslide.' Inside fashion's worrying runway trend
events.description: Now that the Fall-Winter 2023 catwalks have been disassembled, it's clear one trend was more pervasive than any collective penchant for ruffles, pleated skirts or tailored coats.

events.title: Michael Jordan's 1998 NBA Finals sneakers sell for a record $2.2 million
events.description: In 1998, Michael Jordan laced up a pair of his iconic black and red Air Jordan 13s to bring home a Bulls victory during Game 2 of his final NBA championship — and now they are the most expensive sneakers ever to sell at auction. The game-winning sneakers sold for $2.2 million at Sotheby's in New York on Tuesday, smashing the sneaker auction record of $1.47 million, set in 2021 by a pair of Nike Air Ships that Jordan wore earlier in his career.

events.title: Here's the real reason to turn on airplane mode when you fly
events.description: We all know the routine by heart: "Please ensure your seats are in the upright position, tray tables stowed, window shades are up, laptops are stored in the overhead bins and electronic devices are set to flight mode."

events.title: 'I was up to my waist down a hippo's throat.' He survived, and here's his advice
events.description: Paul Templer was living his best life.

events.title: Relaxed entry rules make it easier than ever to visit this stunning Asian nation
events.description: Due to its remoteness and short summer season, Mongolia has long been a destination overlooked by travelers.

events.title: The most beautiful sections of China's Great Wall
events.description: Having lived in Beijing for almost 12 years, I've had plenty of time to travel widely in China.

events.title: Sign up to our newsletter for a weekly roundup of travel news

events.title: Congressional Democrats raise alarm over Trump's comments on Iran
events.description: Dozens of congressional Democrats raised alarm Tuesday over President Trump's rhetoric about Iran. Most Republican lawmakers have been silent.

events.title: Norway's Telenor faces lawsuit for giving junta data from its customers in Myanmar
events.description: A Swedish non-profit has filed a class action lawsuit against Norway's Telenor, accusing it of endangering customers in Myanmar by sharing their data with the junta. The company said it had no real choice.

events.title: JD Vance criticizes Zelenskyy and Ukraine in Hungary on visit campaigning for Viktor Orban
events.description: US Vice President JD Vance has criticized EU leaders and Ukraine's Volodymyr Zelenskyy during his visit to Hungary, echoing Prime Minister Viktor Orban's campaign messaging. The Kremlin chimed in from Moscow, too.

events.title: Taiwan opposition leader Cheng Li-wun makes rare China visit
events.description: During a visit to a memorial for Taiwan's founding father in Nanjing, KMT leader Cheng Li-wun called for "reconciliation and unity" with Beijing. Taiwan's ruling democrats say China needs to stop its aggression.

events.title: Should Western influencers promote Taliban‑run Afghanistan?
events.description: In the race for clicks, likes and followers, some travel content creators are being criticized for promoting Afghanistan while ignoring the harsh reality of Taliban rule.

events.title: Afghanistan, Pakistan held 'useful' peace talks, Kabul says
events.description: Clashes between the Afghan Taliban and Pakistan reignited in February. Pakistan accuses Afghanistan of harboring hostile militants, which Kabul denies. China has offered to mediate in easing tensions.

events.title: Virtual Romanian singer goes viral but sparks criticism
events.description: In Romania, an AI-generated singer inspired by poems about the Roma community has become an overnight success. But critics say her creator is monetizing the woes of a marginalized group without involving its members.

events.title: Trump's surprise claim in his call with Sky News
events.description: 👉 Follow Trump100 on your podcast app 👈

events.title: Greece to ban under-15s from social media
events.description: Greece will ban under-15s from social media from next year.

events.title: Scottish 'crime boss' extradited after Bali arrest
events.description: An alleged Scottish crime boss wanted by Spain has been extradited from Bali, Indonesian authorities have confirmed.

events.title: Afghanistan says peace talks held in China to end fighting with Pakistan have been constructive
events.description: Afghanistan’s Foreign Ministry says peace talks held in China between Afghanistan and Pakistan to halt cross-border fighting between the two neighbors have been constructive

events.title: Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney attracts yet another Conservative lawmaker to his Liberal Party
events.description: Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney has attracted another opposition Conservative lawmaker to the Liberal Party, further assuring that he will soon have a majority government

events.title: British pastor charged with manslaughter after man drowns at baptism
events.description: A British pastor faces manslaughter charges in the drowning of a 61-year-old man during a backyard baptism

events.title: Activists ring alarm bells about halt in Poland's air pollution progress
events.description: Poland's "Clean Air" program, aimed at reducing pollution and household heating demand, is losing momentum

events.title: College student accused of letting newborn drown – police reveal phone photos of ‘hated mom’ Casey Anthony
events.description: A grand jury indicted Florida college student Anne Mae Demegillo on first-degree murder after she allegedly watched her newborn drown in a toilet at home.

events.title: Accused HOA swindler on the run after blowing residents’ cash on plastic surgery, luxe trips, shopping: cops
events.description: Police say a South Florida property manager siphoned HOA funds for shopping sprees, plastic surgery and vacations before allegedly vanishing.

events.title: Texas man tackled by church security after bringing loaded gun, ammo to Houston service
events.description: Police say a Texas man armed with a gun and more than 100 rounds of ammunition was tackled by a security guard inside a Houston church before shots.

events.title: Iran's UN ambassador takes swipe at Trump in final hours before Strait of Hormuz deadline
events.description: Iran's UN ambassador is calling one of President Donald Trump's posts on Truth Social on Tuesday “deeply irresponsible" and “profoundly alarming."

events.title: Inside Iran’s ruling ideology: How a ‘holy mission’ and messianic doctrine fuel regime extremism
events.description: Iranian journalist says Iran's regime uses messianic Mahdi doctrine to claim divine authority and make political compromise nearly impossible.
